definition:
1. hot, warm , θερμὰ λοετρά (Homer); of tears, (Homer), etc. 
2. metaphorically hot, hasty, rash, headlong , like Lat. calidus, (Aeschulus Tragicus), etc. 
3. still warm, fresh , ἴχνη [variant datesAnthology Palantina 
4. τὸ θερμόν = θερμότης, heat , Lat. calor, (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 
5. θερμόν (i.e. ὕδωρ); hot water , θερμῷ λοῦσθαι (Aristophanes Comicus) 
6. τὰ θερμά (entry χωρία); (Herdotus Historicus): but (entry λουτρά); hot baths , (Xenophon Historicus) 
7. adverb -μῶς, (Plato Philosophus) (ML)
